name: Margulis Theorem

phase_0.boundary_condition: the space of all possible lattice configurations and their deformation constraints

VINIT.justification: void state before any perturbation; the pristine, uninitialized lattice configuration

TANCH.justification: terminal anchor that closes the system; the boundary condition that contains all valid deformations

AFWD.justification: forward morphism; the directed transformation from one lattice configuration toward another via smooth deformation

AREV.justification: reverse/descent morphism; the contravariant path back to the original lattice, undoing the deformation

CLINK.justification: sequential chaining; applying one deformation after another to build complex transformations

IMSCRIB.justification: self-reference; the lattice recognizing itself under the trivial (no) deformation

FSPLIT.chosen_element: rigidity dichotomy: does the lattice admit non-trivial deformations?
FSPLIT.justification: branches into two paths: (T) rigid lattice with only trivial deformations, (F) flexible lattice with continuous deformation family

FFUSE.chosen_element: reconstruction of the original lattice Λ from its rigidity classification
FFUSE.justification: reconstitutes the input lattice by fusing the rigid and flexible branches; FFUSE(FSPLIT(Λ)) = Λ holds because the rigidity property determines the lattice up to isometry

EVALT.chosen_element: rigidity confirmed: Λ is arithmetically rigid
EVALT.justification: affirmative branch; the lattice admits only trivial deformations (Margulis rigidity holds)

EVALF.chosen_element: rigidity fails: Λ admits non-trivial continuous deformations
EVALF.justification: negative branch; the lattice is flexible, contradicting the rigidity hypothesis

ENGAGR.chosen_element: marginal case: lattice at the boundary between rigidity and flexibility
ENGAGR.justification: paradice state; both rigidity and flexibility are simultaneously held without resolution, as in deformation spaces with measure-zero rigid loci

IFIX.justification: append-only fixation; once a lattice is classified as rigid or flexible, this determination is irreversible and becomes part of the mathematical record

phase_2.split_input: lattice Λ with its geometric and arithmetic properties
phase_2.fuse_result: original lattice Λ, recovered from its rigidity type and geometric invariants

phase_3.void_description: 00 — uninitialized lattice before any rigidity analysis; no deformation structure yet assigned
phase_3.true_description: 01 — lattice is arithmetically rigid; only trivial deformations exist; Margulis theorem holds
phase_3.false_description: 10 — lattice admits non-trivial continuous deformations; rigidity fails; flexible family exists
phase_3.both_description: 11 — paradice state: lattice simultaneously exhibits rigid and flexible properties, held in superposition at the boundary of deformation space

steps.domain_action: genesis of an undeformed lattice Λ₀ in its pristine, unanalyzed state
apply a candidate deformation φ to the lattice, moving it toward a transformed configuration Λ'
compose multiple deformations φ₂ ∘ φ₁ to build a continuous family of transformations
recognize the identity deformation id as the self-reference point; the lattice unchanged
branch into rigidity dichotomy — does the lattice admit only trivial deformations (T) or continuous families (F)?
T-branch affirmed — lattice is rigid; only trivial deformations exist; arithmetic rigidity holds
on the F-branch, apply inverse deformation φ⁻¹ to reverse any transformation and test flexibility
F-branch affirmed — lattice is flexible; non-trivial continuous deformations exist; rigidity fails
at the boundary, both rigidity and flexibility are simultaneously held without resolution; paradice state
reconstitute the original lattice Λ from its rigidity classification; FFUSE(FSPLIT(Λ)) = Λ
permanently record the rigidity classification; this determination is irreversible and becomes canonical
close the system with the rigidity constraint ρ(Λ) = constant; the boundary that contains all valid deformations

phase_5.compiler_frontend: Margulis rigidity theorem itself — translates the geometric and arithmetic properties of a lattice into a rigidity verdict
phase_5.ipc_mechanism: deformation family communication — rigid and flexible branches exchange information through the rigidity invariant
phase_5.memory_mechanism: the lattice's arithmetic and geometric invariants (determinant, discriminant, automorphism group) stored as canonical data
phase_5.scheduler_mechanism: the order of deformation application and rigidity testing; determined by the lattice's dimension and arithmetic complexity
phase_5.alfs_store: the space of all lattices in ℝⁿ, indexed by their rigidity type and deformation family

phase_6.cycle_cost: the computational cost of rigidity verification; proportional to the lattice dimension and complexity of the deformation family
phase_6.pre_cycle_state: unclassified lattice Λ with unknown rigidity status; maximum entropy in deformation space
phase_6.post_cycle_state: classified lattice with determined rigidity type; entropy reduced by the rigidity verdict
phase_6.delta_s_verdict: ΔS ≈ 0 — the rigidity classification is a pure information operation; no energy is dissipated, only geometric structure is revealed

lean_scaffold:
-- IGProtocol scaffold: VINIT → AFWD → CLINK → IMSCRIB → FSPLIT → EVALT → AREV → EVALF → ENGAGR → FFUSE → IFIX → TANCH
-- Class: Margulis Theorem
-- Fingerprint: sig=(6,2,3,1)
--   self_ref=False | frobenius_order=1
--   dialetheia_complete=True | period=12
-- Expected tier: O₂
-- FSPLIT/FFUSE pairs: [(4, 9)]

import Imscribing.IGMorphism
import Imscribing.IGFunctor

namespace Imscribing
open Primitives Frobenius IGProtocol
open Dimensionality Topology Relational Polarity Grammar
     Fidelity KineticChar Granularity Criticality Protection Stoichiometry Chirality

-- ── Token → IG field mapping ──────────────────────────────────────────────
--   [0] VINIT     dim    := 𐑼               𐑼 → 𐑾  | initial object — ground of distinction
--   [1] AFWD      rel    := 𐑾               𐑼 → 𐑱  | forward morphism — bidirectional arrow
--   [2] CLINK     fid    := 𐑱               𐑾 → 𐑠  | composition — regime coherence
--   [3] IMSCRIB   gram   := 𐑠               𐑱 → 𐑚  | identity — self-imscription
--   [4] FSPLIT    gran   := 𐑚               𐑚 → 𐑚  | split δ — range decomposition
--   [5] EVALT     crit   := ⊙               𐑚 → 𐑙  | evaluate-true — criticality gate open
--   [6] AREV      pol    := 𐑗               𐑚 → 𐑙  | reverse morphism — parity flip
--   [7] EVALF     chir   := 𐑖               𐑚 → 𐑙  | evaluate-false — chirality check
--   [8] ENGAGR    stoi   := 𐑳               𐑚 → 𐑙  | engage paradox — B-state, both arms
--   [9] FFUSE     stoi   := 𐑙               𐑙 → 𐑭  | fuse μ — assembly mode
--   [10] IFIX      prot   := 𐑭               𐑙 → 𐑡  | irreversible fixation — winding number
--   [11] TANCH     top    := 𐑡               𐑭 → 𐑼  | terminal object — connectivity boundary

-- ── Main IGProtocol term ────────────────────────────────────────────────────

noncomputable def margulis_theorem_protocol : IGProtocol 𐑼 𐑡 :=
  .withGram 𐑠 <|
  -- Seq chain:
  (.arrow 𐑼 𐑼 𐑾)  -- [0] VINIT | dim := 𐑼 | initial object — ground of distinction (genesis of an undeformed lattice Λ₀ in its pristine, unanalyzed state)
  (.arrow 𐑾 𐑼 𐑱)  -- [1] AFWD | rel := 𐑾 | forward morphism — bidirectional arrow (apply a candidate deformation φ to the lattice, moving it toward a transforme...)
  (.arrow 𐑱 𐑾 𐑠)  -- [2] CLINK | fid := 𐑱 | composition — regime coherence (compose multiple deformations φ₂ ∘ φ₁ to build a continuous family of transfo...)
  (.arrow 𐑠 𐑱 𐑚)  -- [3] IMSCRIB | gram := 𐑠 | identity — self-imscription (recognize the identity deformation id as the self-reference point; the lattic...)
  -- FSPLIT [4] (gran := 𐑚) (branch into rigidity dichotomy — does the lattice admit only trivial deformations (T) or continuous families (F)?) / FFUSE [9] (stoi := 𐑙)
  .seq
    (.prod
      -- T-branch (2 nodes)
      .seq
        (.arrow ⊙ 𐑚 𐑙)  -- [5] EVALT | crit := ⊙ | evaluate-true — criticality gate open (T-branch affirmed — lattice is rigid; only trivial deformations exist; arithm...)
        (.arrow 𐑗 𐑚 𐑙)  -- [6] AREV | pol := 𐑗 | reverse morphism — parity flip (on the F-branch, apply inverse deformation φ⁻¹ to reverse any transformation ...)
      -- F-branch (2 nodes)
      .seq
        (.arrow 𐑖 𐑚 𐑙)  -- [7] EVALF | chir := 𐑖 | evaluate-false — chirality check (F-branch affirmed — lattice is flexible; non-trivial continuous deformations ...)
        (.arrow 𐑳 𐑚 𐑙)  -- [8] ENGAGR | stoi := 𐑳 | engage paradox — B-state, both arms (at the boundary, both rigidity and flexibility are simultaneously held withou...))
    -- reconnect at FFUSE [9]: μ closes the Frobenius pair
    (.arrow 𐑙 𐑙 𐑭)  -- [9] FFUSE | stoi := 𐑙 (reconstitute the original lattice Λ from its rigidity classification; FFUSE(FSPLIT(Λ)) = Λ)
  (.arrow 𐑭 𐑙 𐑡)  -- [10] IFIX | prot := 𐑭 | irreversible fixation — winding number (permanently record the rigidity classification; this determination is irrever...)
  (.arrow 𐑡 𐑭 𐑼)  -- [11] TANCH | top := 𐑡 | terminal object — connectivity boundary (close the system with the rigidity constraint ρ(Λ) = constant; the boundary t...)

-- ── Evaluation arm sub-defs ─────────────────────────────────────────────────

-- truth arm
noncomputable def margulis_theorem_true_arm : IGProtocol 𐑼 𐑡 :=
  (margulis_theorem_protocol).restrictToEVALT

-- false arm
noncomputable def margulis_theorem_false_arm : IGProtocol 𐑼 𐑡 :=
  (margulis_theorem_protocol).restrictToEVALF

-- ── Verification theorems ───────────────────────────────────────────────────

theorem margulis_theorem_tier : TierFunctor.obj 𐑼 = .O₂ := by decide

-- Frobenius (split → fuse): μ∘δ = id on .prod branch
-- Proof: apply igFrobAlg_self_fusion; exact mu_delta_A_id
-- (requires mu_delta_A_id from IGFunctor library)

end Imscribing
